IT HISTORY
Two decades before venture capital existed as an industry — a Stanford professor was already personally underwriting his students’ startups

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

creator genenrate by arkSilicon Valley has a brain, and it sits on an old horse farm in Palo Alto. For more than a century, the people, patents, and companies that run the tech industry — Google, Cisco, Sun, Yahoo, LinkedIn, Instagram, and tens of thousands more — have come out of Stanford University. This is the story of how that brain got built, in five parts.

Silicon Valley Brain

The HP Garage, 367 Addison Avenue. CC BY-SA 2.0, via Wikimedia Commons.A Sears Drill in the Trunk
In 1938, David Packard drove back to California with his new wife, Lucile, and a Sears Roebuck electric drill rattling in the trunk.
That drill was more than a household tool — it became HP’s first piece of production equipment…
